4ιε´
Three lines of Greek letters, seemingly not centered / regularly laid out.

Described by Orsi (1896) as 'scritto a pessimi caratteri', and (1899) as 'lettere cattive, rustiche cosi che a tutta prima sembrano archaiche, e molto sconservate... L'iscrizione negligentemente redatta e scolpita è funebre di tarda età'. Pace states 'lettere del III sec. av. Cr.', but given the presence of lunate epsilon and Orsi's comments, a later date seems much more likely.
Suggested to be of later Roman date by Orsi on the basis of lettering etc.

1Ἀρτεμοι̣

Archaic, on the basis of the lettering.

1 Πριγομένια {²⁶Πριμογένια}²⁶ χρησ-
2 [τ]ὰ {²⁶χρηστὴ}²⁶ κὲ ἄμεμπτο<ς > κὲ
3 [πι]στὴ κ̣ [ὲ] ἀμίμη-
4 {μη}τος {²⁶ἀμίμητος}²⁶, ἔζησε ἔτη [— —].
5
The text extends across the full surviving width of the middle band of the front face.

Slightly unevenly spaced deeply v-cut letters. Delta has a slight apex; Mu has vertical first and last, middle strokes only descend half-way; Nu has lower right angle slightly above the line; omicron is full size; rhos has small closed, curved eye; sigma has prallel top and bottom strokes, with relatively shallow mid-strokes; phi has large rhomboid body, traversed by the vertical which is extended (120 mm); omega is open, rounded, with long horizontal tails, and is slightly smaller than the full line-height (75-85 mm).
Photo J. Prag 26.04.2022: Aut. Assessorato Beni Culturali Regione Siciliana n.10681 del 06/05/2014
